allow: Bash(DRY_RUN=true EPIC="認証・アカウント管理" bash:*)
Bash(EPIC="認証・アカウント管理" bash:*)

hooks.prompt:
On session start, sync docs/issues/ to GitHub issues for this repo (ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge).

Workflow:
1. Read docs/superpowers/plans/ to understand current implementation work
2. Find all issue.md files under docs/issues/ recursively that have YAML frontmatter with `github_issue:`
3. For each file:
   a. If `github_issue` is a number: compare the markdown body (below the frontmatter) with the current GitHub issue body via `gh issue view <number> --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --json body -q .body`. If different, update with `gh issue edit <number> --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --body '<content>'`
   b. If `github_issue` is null or missing: create a new GitHub issue using the title from frontmatter and the markdown body, then write the assigned issue number back into the frontmatter
4. Maintain parent-child hierarchy: if an issue.md is inside a subfolder that also has an issue.md, link the child issue to the parent in the body

Only process files that have `github_issue:` in their frontmatter. Leave other files untouched.

A file in docs/issues/ was just written or edited: check the tool input for the file path.

1. Read the changed file
2. Check if it has YAML frontmatter with `github_issue:`
3. If `github_issue` is a number: update the corresponding GitHub issue body with `gh issue edit <number> --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --body '<markdown body below frontmatter>'`
4. If `github_issue` is null: create a new GitHub issue with the title from frontmatter and write the number back into the file's frontmatter
5. If no `github_issue` key exists: do nothing

A git commit was just made. Do the following:
1. Run `git log -1 --format='%H %s'` to get the commit hash and message
2. Run `gh issue view 3 --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --json body` to read the current issue body
3. Based on the commit message, determine which task(s) in the issue were completed
4. Post a comment to the issue using `gh issue comment 3 --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --body '...'` with the progress update. The comment should include: the completed task name, the commit message, and the commit hash as a reference
5. Also update the issue body to check off the completed task(s): change `- [ ]` to `- [x]` for matching tasks using `gh issue edit 3 --repo ebi-yu/refactoring-konowledge --body '...'`

Only act on tasks that clearly match the commit message. If nothing matches, do nothing.
hooks.statusMessage: GitHub issue の進捗を更新中...
